string 2:
Thank you for coming, and for your aid at the radio tower.
Please accept my gratitude once more.

I owe you my thanks as well.
You're quite the incredible bunch.
And...
...What is it?
No, never mind.

At any rate, you have done so much for us.
Now it is our turn to hear what we can do for you.

...but forgive me. The truth is,
a new problem has arisen.
A new problem?{next}
When can we go back to our world?{next}
Trouble seems to love you.{next}

After yesterday, we tried communicating with various places
without much success. The Titan invasion must be spreading.

We need Junomon's power to send you back to your world,
but the Titan invasion is spreading faster than we expected.

A love most undesired... Perhaps that is why the Titan
invasion is spreading more swiftly than expected.
As things stand, we cannot get you to Junomon's temple.

Normally, we use the Locomon Railroad to reach Junomon's temple,
but trouble is afoot at the Factorial Area that powers it.
What should we do?{next}
The Locomon Railroad?
The Factorial Area?
I will clear out the Titans who have entered our fair city.

As for all of you... Perhaps you are the best candidates
to go to the Factorial Area for me?
A series of railroad lines connecting this world.

Normally, it could take you to Junomon's temple, but sadly
it is no longer receiving power, rendering it nonoperational.
First, power must be restored to the Factorial Area.

The area that is home to the reactor that single-handedly
powers the entire Digital World of Iliad.

Until it is restored, use of the Locomon Railroad is
impossible—as is safe travel to Junomon's temple.
All right. We'll do it.{next}
Sounds like you're trusting us with a lot.
You can wire my fees to my agency account.

I apologize for the trouble, but you have my thanks.
Once peace is restored, I promise to return you to your world.
...Sure. Sounds good!

The more I learn about this world, the more I feel like
I find out who I am.
Aww, Aegiomon...

I have prepared a temporary means of reaching the Factorial
Area. Please assemble on the main thoroughfare.

Now, then. I ask that you all be very careful
on this operation.
Minervamon has told me in detail about each of you.

It just might be... that you in particular are the
best one for this job.
H-Huh? Me?

There's something... interesting about you. It's another
thing I'd like to check with Junomon about right away.

I apologize, but the only reward we can offer
is a solemn promise to return you to your world.

That, and... maybe some information about you.
But that'll have to wait until I can check in with Junomon.

Either way, nothing's happening until the situation
at the Factorial Area gets fixed.
So please, lend us a hand.

Whoa, awesome! What is that?
It's super huge! Can we ride it?
No way! You guys are the ones that're awesome!
I heard ya took out the Titan at the radio tower, huh!

Sounds like a big stew of trouble is brewing in the
Digital World... so we gotta do our part, too!

I'll give ya all a lift to the Factorial Area, so make sure
ya send those nasty Titans packing, ya hear?

Okay, off we go! All aboard, everybody!
Blimpmon, ready for liftoff!
This Factorial Area... I wonder what it's like.

The Factorial Area is the energy source for the
whole Digital World of Iliad!

But word is the Titans attacked there, too, so it
might be in worse shape than we think!

We won't know until we get there, though...
That's why I'm flying extra careful!
Eek!

That sounded like artillery fire!
I think it's coming from... the ground!
What?! F-From the ground? What's going on?!
Maybe the Titans are invading quicker than we thought.
GAAAGH! That was a direct hit...!
Sorry, folks, but I gotta make an emergency landing!
AAAAAAAAAGH!
Ow...
Is everyone okay?
F-For now, anyway... So this is the Factorial Area, huh?
What was that attack just now? Was it the Titans?
The situation here might be way worse than we thought...

Wonder if anybody in the village knows what's going on?
The village is right there, could you guys go take a look?
Who are you? More intruders?!
Merukimon sent us.{next}
I'm an agent of a certain organization.
Duty demands I keep my identity secret.
Merukimon? Really?

That's a relief! I heard we got a signal again for a second,
so we must've gotten a message through then.

So how come Merukimon sent you instead of
coming personally?

Titans invaded the town, too... Everyone is scared
and in a huge panic.

Merukimon stayed behind to try and help.
That's why we're here instead.
What...?! I had no idea...

So that means it's not just here, but the entire
Digital World of Iliad that's in trouble?!
What're you talking about?

You're... a human, aren't you?
Why would a human come here?

What business would humans have in this place...?
How do I know you aren't intruders?!
That just makes you even more suspicious...

Regardless, what could you possibly want at a time like this...?
What proof do you have you aren't intruders?!

The Titans have seized a portion of the Factorial Area's
central control system.

With some of our people running amok and all the closed
bulkheads, there's no getting down there at present!
We've also lost contact with Lord Vulcanusmon!
What's happening in the Factorial Area?{next}
What's Iliad again?
That's right. It's a huge crisis!

The Titans are attacking! Probably it's the same in other
areas, too!

And they've seized a portion of Factorial Town's
central control system. But the biggest issue of all...

...is the fact we've lost contact with Vulcanusmon, who
directly oversees the reactor!

That's the true name of this world! Although, most of the
time, everyone just calls it "the Digital World."
And this Factorial Area here is home to Iliad's power source!

I thought it was audacious enough the Titans were attacking
here, but this isn't even their only target...

I see! That certainly explains why Merukimon is too busy
to help over here.

Each of us Digimon living in the Digital World has to stand
together and face this threat head-on!

We can't let the Titans take the Factorial Area, the precious
source of our world's power!

So, the Titans are here, too... This is just what we were
afraid of! Who's this "Vulcanusmon"?

Our boss, who directly controls the power for this area.
But... recently Vulcanusmon stopped sending out power.

He'll be in the power room on the lowest level of this area. 
Since the comms are out, you'll have to talk to him directly.

But the biggest obstacle are the Titans who've already
gotten inside.
At present, it's impossible to reach the area's lowest level!

So you're saying if we could make it to the bottom level,
Vulcanusmon could do something about the power?
Yes, most likely.

If we restore the power here and then meet Junomon, we
could get Inori home...
...and we'd be able to help everyone out in this world...
It'll make everyone happy... so we have to go, right?
Yeah!
S-Seriously?! You can actually fight the Titans?

D-Don't you worry! After all, we're the ones who ran the
Titans out of Central Town!

Well, but I suppose you are envoys of Merukimon. Maybe you
can manage it. In which case, please! We need your help!

You'll need the help of Kokuwamon here in the village to 
reach the lowest level, but it might not be amenable now.
How come?

To reach the lowest level, first you'll have to get the help of 
Kokuwamon in the village.

Kokuwamon operates the bulkheads, but apparently overdid
things and got injured in the evacuation.

Kokuwamon said they were waiting for repairs, a little ways
down from here by the first bulkhead.
That's worrying...
We should go make sure Kokuwamon is doing all right.
We're going to have to ask you folks for your help.

First things first. Go find Kokuwamon. Meanwhile, you can
count on us to repair Blimpmon!

An energy crisis during a time of war, huh? It seems the
Digimon society isn't so different from our own...

We're having trouble securing a supply of power
in the post-Shinjuku Inferno world, too.

Once our current supplies dry up, it's game over... You need
to find a way to rewrite the future before that time comes.

I'm Kokuwamon! I'm sorry I can't get up to help right now.
I'm currently out of order.

I can't do anything in this state, but I don't have the part I
need to repair myself, either.
Where's this part at?{next}
Guardromon told us to come see you.
What put you out of commission?

The part we need, a fuse, is a valuable and rare part that's
difficult to come by in these parts.

If anyone has one, it would have to be the merchants who
come to this settlement from elsewhere.

But as you can see, I am unfortunately unable to move, let
alone get up and head to the merchants for the part we need.

The station is outside the village, at the top of the hill. With
any luck, you'll find the part there...
The top of the hill, huh?
That's just back the way we came a short distance, right?
Yeah! Come on, Inori and {player}! Let's go have a look!
I see.

If Guardromon allowed you to enter, then you must be safe,
trustworthy travelers.

Well, you see, I'm in charge of opening and closing the
bulkheads in this area.

I had to forcibly close the bulkheads to prevent any more
of our comrades from going berserk.

In doing so I overloaded my circuits, blew a fuse, and now
I am unable to move.

I'll be fine as soon as I get the right part. I'm just waiting for it
to be delivered now.

Then we'll head to the merchants for you! We'll ask if they
have this "fuse."

Really? That would be wonderful. If you can get me that
fuse, I'll be up and running in no time.
I feel terrible about asking our guests for such a favor...
...but please. We need your help.
Now, let's be on our way! The bulkhead is at the bottom of the hill!
Let's go, Inori and {player}!
Hello there. Want to buy something?
Do you have a fuse?{next}
Who are you?
What do you have?
A Fuse? I just sold one for cheap!
They never sell and just take up space in my inventory.
That was my last one! So I'm all out.
Just a wandering salesman! Though, I'm not really wandering anymore.
These are some troubled times, so I've opened up shop right here.

I've got everything! From your daily needs, to things you
didn't even know you needed!
Some of my items are super unique, so don't miss out!
But how peculiar...

Fuses almost never sell, but here are two customers in a 
row asking for one.
Is there nothing you can do?
I'm afraid not... I don't have any coming in anytime soon, either.
Oh, don't be so gloomy! Here, have this!
This also isn't too popular. And no, I can't deny it's leftover stock.

But one Digimon's trash is another Digimon's treasure! You never
know what'll end up being useful down the line.

I've got all sorts of other items too, so if you've ever got the
chance, come see more of my wares!
WOOOOOOO! YEAH BABY, THAT'S WHAT I'VE BEEN WAITING FOR!
I'm buzzing! Crackling! Bursting with life!
Maybe that was a little too much electricity...
Pardon me, I got a little too excited for a second there.
Everyone, thank you so much! I'm good as new now!
I heard from Guardromon that you all want to meet with Vulcanusmon?
Yup. Could you take us to him if you're feeling better?
Actually, I can't just yet.

I mentioned that I closed the bulkheads to prevent
the madness from spreading.

Beyond the bulkheads, you'll find not only my berserk
allies, but stable ones as well who got trapped in there.

I wanted to wait until everyone managed to escape,
but to prevent a total disaster, I had no choice...

Your friends are still inside... That must have been
a hard decision.
...Indeed, it was.
Among our colleagues trapped behind the bulkhead is my partner.

Without our powers combined, we cannot enter the lowest level
of the reactor, where Vulcanusmon resides.
So we gotta go save your partner from behind these bulkheads?

You were able to help me, so I have full faith in your ability to help
my partner as well. Could you please help all the trapped Digimon?

I want to accompany you but it's still dangerous to leave
the bulkheads open for long periods of time.
I must stay here and close the doors once you're all inside.

You got it, leave it to us! We'll help your partner and all the 
other Digimon too!
Oh, thank you!
The Titans are wreaking havoc on the other side of this wall.

I'll open the bulkheads when you're ready, so let me know when
you've finished preparations.

Doesn't look like it's gonna open.
Maybe Kokuwamon can help?
...Um, we have a situation! The switch is broken!
Damn! There's nothing we can do if the switch is busted...
Isn't there any other way to the reactor?

There's no other route, unfortunately. And it'll take
some time to repair this switch.
Hmm. This is a real pickle...
Wh-What was that?
Careful, everyone!
It's... enormous...!
Wh-What is that thing? One of your friends...?
I'm afraid not!
No one in our village is this humongous!
So it really is one of those Titans, huh?!
Here it comes!
Whew. Thank goodness...

If we stand around here too long, another Titan might come
attack...

We definitely have to hurry, but we can't open the
bulkhead...
What are you doing, Aegiomon?

Hey... maybe we can get down there by climbing down this
Digimon's body.
Considering how huge it is...
...that could work!
Way to go, Aegiomon!

It might not be the safest way, but I think it's our only
ticket down.
Then let's go! Everyone, watch your step!

Gaaagh! Now there are Titans over here!
And more Titans over there!

No amount of hands is enough to deal with
all this! Gaaagh!
Oh, no! Titans, here?!
No! We're—

Don't try to deny it! This area is off-limits to
regular citizens! No one is allowed inside!

And yet here you are! So that means you're
unauthorized intruders! Enemies! Titans!

With this defense system, Divine Arms Version Ω,
I'll have you fleeing with your tails tucked between your legs!
Y-You've got it all wrong! We aren't Titans—
Whoa!
Aegiomon!
Inori!

No one lays a finger on Inori!
I swore to keep her safe!
First things first! Those arms need to go!
Here they come!
How's this!

How can this be...?
My Divine Arms...

Is this where it all ends...?
At the very least...
...let me try to save my treasure...!
Ngh...!
Hm?

GAAAAAAAAAGH! Nooo, this can't beee!
My precious treasuuure!

I-It's all over! First my Divine Arms,
then my treasure! And now my life!

Come on, then! If you're going to kill me, then do it!
You Titan assassins! There's nothing more you can do to me!
Calm down, already...{next}
What's with that action figure?{next}
I don't take unnecessary lives.{next}

How can I possibly be calm?!
I've lost everything!

"Action figure"? What is that gibberish you speak?
This is my prized treasure, the treasure I found myself!

And now it, too, is lost to me...
Torn from me like everything else!

Gah! Not only do I lose all I hold dear,
but I have to face humiliation, too?!

If it's come to this, I'll have to settle things
with my own hands!

Isn't that one of those things they sell
a bunch of in Akihabara...?

Wait. Really? You mean to say you've
beheld such wonders before?
Um, well, yeah... back in the human world...
The human world...?!
That's the world we came from, after all.

What? Then that means...
...you people aren't... Titans?
That's what we've been trying to tell you.
Ohhh. Oh. Ah, well... Sorry.
How incredibly disrespectful of me.

...How are things outside?
A bit more stable?

We've come to restore power to the Digital World.
Word is you can help do something about that.

Well, it's true that I did cut off the power supply
to prevent the Titans from making use of it...

...but the fact that you're here must mean that something's
put the brakes on their incursion somewhat.

And I imagine you have need of electric
power now... That about right?

Yeah! We heard we need Junomon's power
to get these guys back to the human world.

And Merukimon said we have to get power to
the railroad first if we want to go see Junomon.
All right, then... I understand!

I've restored power to all of Iliad. That should
get the railroad up and running again.

You should be able to use the elevator right outside now.
It'll take you right back up to the village.

As for the rest, we'll handle things here.
You folks have your own road to travel.
Thank you so much, Mr... Vulcanusmon?
No need for "Mr."! Just "Vulcanusmon" is fine!

So, will you take the elevator up and explain
things to everyone back in the village for me?

Then, this is goodbye! I look forward to
seeing you all again someday!

You know... I'd love to visit the human world some day.
Especially if they have wondrous things like my treasure there!

All aboard the Locomon Railroad! Just tell us
where you want to go, and we'll get you there!

Locomon will pull into the station momentarily.
Please wait until they come to a complete stop!

So, in this world... it sounds like the Digimon are fighting
each other... and tooth and nail, too...
I guess my dad was right all along.

Three years ago, when my mom and little brother died, my
dad launched his own investigation into what happened.
One day, I secretly read through all his notes.

He claimed they'd been caught up in some sort of conflict... 
between phase electron lifeforms.
I bet he was probably right on the money.

This clash between the Digimon and Titans isn't their problem alone.
It affected our world, too—harmed it, even.

We can't let any more people get hurt by this madness. Digimon get sad
when they lose their friends too, right?

I wish I could bring peace to the Digital World... Then nothing like this
would ever happen again.

What she says about the relationship between the two worlds
is the same as what we gleaned from the Shinjuku Inferno.

Make no mistake. That anomalous incident was the result of
this Digimon conflict reaching a fever pitch.

And, if so, then nipping this conflict in the bud in the
Digital World is the only way to prevent future tragedies.
...Keep that in mind, all right, Agent {player}?

Welcome back. It seems the railroad is once again operational.
Now you may venture to Junomon's Temple.

I'll go along with you guys. I'll introduce you... and make
a report, while I'm at it.
Okay, let's head out.{next}
Report? What kind of report?{next}
I guess it's time to say goodbye to Merukimon.{next}

Yeah, let's head to Junomon's temple, the Guardian
Palace, from the station.
Oh, just talking to myself. Don't worry about it.

I suppose you are right, yes. You have all truly been a
great help. I pray you make it back to your world safely.

What about you, Aegiomon? You are a Digimon, after all,
so you of course may stay if you so desire.
Me?

Humans are best suited to their own world, and
the same is true for Digimon as well.
Is the human world not a difficult place for you to live in?

I heard that you were even forced to conceal your true 
appearance there...

I do not expect you to make your decision here and now.
I suggest you think long and hard on the matter.

For now, let's head to the Guardian Palace. I imagine Junomon
might have some unique insights on the matter, too.
